meaning: adj.沉重缓慢的,单调乏味的
examples: He is plodding on with negotiations.
I watched her plodding her way across the field.
Round a bend in the canal came plodding a solitary horse, stooping forward as if in anxious thought.
Collison is a plodding, uninspired piece of "faction" covering those same events of 32 years ago.
Yet across the Rhine the preference is for plodding progress rather than grands projets.
